sentences.text: Jordan was twenty-two years old when she began her first job.
She graduated from college with honors last week.
She worked hard and felt proud of her effort.
She studied late at night and felt very tired.
She earned her degree and felt very happy.
She is ready to show that she can do it.
She is ready to succeed and feels good.
But on her first day, Jordan saw something new.
She was not like the others and that made her special.
She was the only woman in a room with many men.
She was the only person with a different skin color.
She was the only one who needed help for her disability.
The men looked at her with doubt in their eyes.
They looked at her and thought she did not belong.
They looked at her like she was wrong and not good.
Jordan tried to not think about it.
She focuses on her work and does not stop.
She tried very hard and did her best.
She arrives early and waits for her friends.
She stayed late and worked on her project.
She asked many questions and wanted to learn more.
She learned all she could and tried her best.
But it was still not enough for Jordan.
The men treated her differently and it was not fair.
They still do not believe her.
They still make her feel small and sad.
One day, Jordan worked at her desk and heard laughter.
She looked up at the sky and smiled.
Her coworkers stand in a circle and laugh at something.
Jordan wants to join them.
She wanted to join the group and be with friends.
When she walked over, the laughter stopped and everyone was quiet.
The men look at her and smile.
They looked uncomfortable and did not smile.
They did not invite her to join the group.
They did not tell us the joke and we did not laugh.
They walked away quickly and did not look back.
Jordan sat down again at her desk.
She felt sad when her heart broke.
She felt heavy because she was different.
She felt sad when they did not include her.
She hears four sad words in her mind: you are left out.
Jordan feels sad and wants to cry.
But she did not do it.
She worked hard and did not stop.
She tried many times and did not give up.
She kept showing that she is good.
But the unfair treatment did not stop.
It happened many times and it was not good.
One afternoon, Jordan's boss called her to his office.
He told her a customer complained.
The customer called her bad names and made her upset.
The customer said she was not good enough.
The customer asked for another person.
Jordan felt her face get hot and she was embarrassed.
She feels her hands shake when she is nervous.
She did nothing wrong and felt good about it.
She only tried to help her friend.
The supervisor said she should handle it better.
He told her she should be more professional.
He said she must work harder.
Jordan wanted to cry because he felt very sad.
But she did not do it.
She nodded and showed that she understood.
She said that she understands what happened.
She went back to her desk and worked harder.
Jordan needed help because of her disability.
She needed special tools to talk and share ideas.
She needed help to understand what people say.
But her job does not understand her.
They did not know how to help her and felt sad.
They did not know what help Sarah needed.
Jordan did not do it either.
She learned new things as she went along.
She tries different ways to solve the problem.
She found what worked for her.
Jordan brought a special keyboard and used it at work.
It let people send messages that she reads right away.
She hoped it would help her talk to her coworkers.
She hoped it would help her feel part of the group.
At first, people were afraid to use it but then tried.
They felt uncomfortable and did not like it.
They did not know how to speak with her.
One day, a coworker named Marcus came to her desk.
He looks nervous and feels worried.
He asked to try the keyboard.
Jordan smiled and said yes to the question.
Marcus types a message on his phone.
Jordan read it right away.
She typed back quickly and felt happy.
They had a talk and shared their ideas.
It was slow and took a long time.
It was not the same as before.
But it worked well and made everyone happy.
Marcus smiled and felt happy.
He said it was nice and fun.
He said he will tell others about it.
More people slowly start to use the keyboard.
They began to talk to Jordan.
They included her in talks and she felt happy.
Jordan feels hope.
She felt happy because she finally connected with others.
She felt happy because she belonged.
But the unfair treatment continued in other ways.
Jordan wants a new job and applied for a promotion.
She had more experience than all the others.
She has better skills and more experience than others.
She proves herself many times and shows her strength.
But she was not chosen for the job.
A man with less experience got the job.
Jordan learned he made thirty-two thousand dollars each year.
She made twenty-eight thousand dollars last year.
She was there longer, but she still felt alone.
She has more education, but she feels unhappy.
She did more work, but she feels tired.
Jordan feels angry.
She feels frustrated because things do not go well.
She felt tired and wanted to give up.
But she did not do it.
She makes a complaint about the problem.
She fought for what she wanted and deserved.
It took a long time of many months.
It takes courage to do difficult things.
It needs hard work and patience to succeed.
But she won the game.
She got the job and felt very happy.
She got the raise and feels very happy.
She showed that she will not be quiet.
Jordan wants to go back to school now.
She wanted to learn more and understand better.
She wants to get better and feels hopeful.
She wanted to show that she was part of the group.
She applied to graduate school and hopes to study more.
She was happy because she was accepted.
She worked in the day and studied at night.
It was difficult and made me feel tired.
It was very tiring and made me feel weak.
But she did it and felt happy.
She earned her master's degree and felt very happy.
But even with her degree, she still faced discrimination.
One day, Jordan walks into a building when a man pushes her.
He opened the door for a woman instead.
Jordan felt very surprised and did not know what to say.
She felt hurt and was very sad.
She feels invisible and sad.
She makes a complaint.
But nothing happened at all.
The man keeps his job and works hard.
Jordan felt alone because he thought no one cared.
Jordan faced discrimination and felt very hurt.
But she continued to fight and did not give up.
She keeps showing that she is good.
She keeps winning and feels very happy.
She gets better at her job every day.
She became a leader and helped many people.
She mentored people who faced discrimination and helped them.
She helped them with the problems they faced.
She helped them and showed them how to speak.
One day, Jordan's company has a big event for people to meet.
It was at a bar in the city.
Jordan feels nervous.
The bars are loud and many people are there.
The bars are crowded and many people are there.
Bars are places where she feels lost and alone.
But she chose to go.
She wanted to meet new people and make friends.
She wanted to make friends and build connections.
She wanted to show that she was part of the group.
Jordan brings her keyboard to the bar.
She puts it on a table.
She waited for her friend to arrive.
People walk by and look around.
They looked at her and smiled.
They looked uncomfortable and did not smile.
But then a person came close.
It was a woman called Sarah.
She was a lawyer and helped many people.
She asked to try the keyboard and play music.
Jordan said yes and smiled.
Sarah types a message.
Jordan read it and then responded.
They had a talk and shared their ideas.
Sarah is interested in many things.
Sarah is respectful and kind to everyone.
Sarah saw that Jordan is important.
Sarah tells Jordan about jobs at her law firm.
She told Jordan about the summer programs and they talked.
She told Jordan that she should apply for a job.
Jordan feels excited.
She felt hopeful and happy about the future.
She felt happy when doors opened for her.
More people come to talk to Jordan.
They used the keyboard and typed many words.
They asked many questions and wanted to know more.
They are curious about many things and want to learn.
They were kind and helped others.
Jordan feels happy because she connects with others.
She felt happy because she belonged with her friends.
She felt happy because she was doing well.
But some people were not kind.
A man came and shook Jordan's hand.
He held it for a long time and felt tired.
He talks and someone types for him.
He said Jordan is inspiring.
Jordan felt bad inside.
People with disabilities often hear "inspiring" but feel it means pity.
Sometimes, when a person feels uncomfortable, they use the wrong words.
Jordan smiled and was polite.
She says thank you to him.
She moved on and felt better.
She found other people to talk to and feel better.
People saw her as a person and not just an inspiration.
People respected her and liked her very much.
People valued her and cared for her feelings.
After the event, Jordan feels happy and full of energy.
She keeps making new friends and growing her social circle.
Coworkers use the keyboard before and after meetings.
New friends in coffee shops say hello and share names.
Sometimes people walk away or say mean things.
Jordan learns to ignore them.
Most people are kind and want to talk in a new way.
Jordan faces discrimination and feels very sad.
But she continued to fight and did not give up.
She keeps showing that she is good.
She keeps succeeding and feels happy about it.
She became a leader and helped many people.
She became a mentor and helped many people.
She helped people who had problems with discrimination.
She made a place where everyone feels welcome and happy.
She built a team that respects each other.
She showed that being different makes them strong.
One day, Jordan speaks at a big conference.
She feels nervous when she talks to new people.
She never spoke in front of many people before.
But she said yes.
She wants to share her story with others.
She wanted to help and inspire other people.
She wanted to show that people can overcome discrimination.
Jordan stood on the stage and looked at the crowd.
She saw many people and they came from different places.
She saw people who faced unfair treatment.
She saw people fighting for change and hope.
She took a deep breath and started to talk.
She tells her story.
She talked about the unfair treatment she faced.
She talked about the times she wanted to give up.
She talked about the people who helped her.
She told how she overcame her problems and felt strong.
She told how she succeeded and felt happy.
When Jordan finished speaking, the audience stood up and clapped.
They clapped and smiled because they were happy.
They cheered loudly and felt very happy.
They thanked her for helping them.
Jordan felt joy in her heart.
She shares her story and feels happy.
She inspired many people and gave them hope.
She showed that people can overcome discrimination.
After the conference, a young woman talks to Jordan.
She cries because she feels sad.
She told Jordan that she also faced unfair treatment.
She told Jordan she wanted to give up.
Hearing Jordan's story gives her hope.
It gave her courage and made her feel strong.
It made her want to fight and not give up.
Jordan hugged the young woman and smiled at her.
She told her that she is not alone.
She told her that she can succeed.
She said that they can overcome discrimination together.
She told her to not give up.
That night, Jordan thought about her trip and felt happy.
She thinks about all the unfairness she faced.
She thinks about all the times she wanted to quit.
She thinks about all the people who doubt her.
But she also thinks about how far she comes.
She thinks about all she has done.
She thought about how many people she helped.
Jordan knows something important now.
The hurt from discrimination made her feel bad.
It made her doubt herself and feel unsure.
It made her feel small and weak.
But it also made her stronger and more brave.
It made her more strong and determined.
It made her kinder and more caring.
It made her who she is now.
Jordan knew that unfair treatment still happens.
She knew there was more work to do.
She knew she would have more problems ahead.
But she knew she could win against them.
She knew she could do well and succeed.
She knew she could help people and make a change.
Jordan started her career and felt invisible.
She feels like she does not belong anymore.
But now she is a leader.
Now she is a mentor and helps others.
Now she is an inspiration for many people.
She overcame discrimination and felt strong.
She proved that she is valuable.
She achieved her dreams and felt very happy.
She was just starting and felt excited.
